{"query":"Bank of Japan inflation projections Iran war energy shock June 2026 statements","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1658,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/economy/policy/articles/boj-may-raise-growth-forecast-230318971.html","title":"BOJ may raise growth forecast, maintain vigilance to inflation risk, sources say","content":"5 days ago — With June's preliminary U.S.-Iran peace deal having triggered sharp falls in oil prices, the board may trim its core inflation forecast … the ...
TOKYO, July 10 (Reuters) - The Bank of Japan may revise up its economic growth forecast for fiscal 2026 and keep its focus on the risk of an inflation overshoot as rising costs from a weak yen and strong AI demand offset some of the ‌declines in oil prices, said three sources familiar with its thinking.
The Middle East war, which began when the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran on February 28, has complicated the BOJ's policy path, stoking inflation through higher oil prices while squeezing an economy dependent on imported fuel.
Even dovish BOJ board member Toichiro Asada said on Monday the pass-through of higher oil prices has been proceeding at a "relatively rapid pace" and could lead to broader price rises across a wide range of goods.
Wholesale inflation spiked ​7.1% in June as firms passed on rising raw material costs.
Both sets of ​data will be available when the BOJ holds its policy ‌meetings in September and October.
The BOJ raised rates to a 31-year high in June in a landmark step in its policy normalisation, signalling readiness to ​tighten further as it focuses on taming price pressures from the energy shock."},{"url":"https://delfineo.com/en/news/20260428-02-boj-rates-iran/","title":"Bank of Japan holds rates and lifts inflation forecast as Iran war fuels energy shock — Delfineo","content":"The Bank of Japan held its benchmark interest rate at about 0.75 per cent on Tuesday, but flagged a sharp rise in inflation as the Iran war drives up energy prices in a country that imports more than 90 per cent of its crude from the Middle East.
The committee lifted its forecast for "core" inflation (consumer prices excluding fresh food) to 2.8 per cent for the fiscal year ending March 2027, up from 1.9 per cent in January, citing higher crude prices feeding through to corporate margins and household real income.
The yen strengthened against the dollar after the decision, but the Nikkei 225 — at an all-time high of 60,537 on Monday — fell about 1 per cent; the bank-heavy Topix rose 0.9 per cent."},{"url":"https://investinglive.com/central-banks/boj-may-raise-2026-growth-forecast-set-to-hold-rates-steady-as-inflation-risks-stay-in-focus","title":"BOJ may raise 2026 growth forecast, set to hold rates steady as inflation risks stay in focus","content":"BOJ may raise 2026 growth forecast, set to hold rates steady as inflation risks stay in focus
The BOJ may trim its core inflation forecast for fiscal 2026 from the 2.8% rise projected in April after the preliminary US-Iran peace deal triggered sharp oil price falls, Reuters reported.
Core consumer inflation, the BOJ's key gauge, stayed below its 2% target for a fourth straight month in May, helped by government subsidies shielding households from rising fuel costs, and the BOJ has said many firms are likely to raise prices for food and daily necessities from summer onward.
The central bank is set to keep its policy rate steady at 1% at its two-day meeting ending July 31, and is likely to retain its vigilance toward inflation risks and broadly maintain its guidance to keep raising rates, though it may avoid giving explicit signals on timing."},{"url":"https://finance.biggo.com/news/02bd0b93-b15e-4eb4-b5e7-2820fc3bd006","title":"One-Year Inflation Expectations Top 90%, Hit Record High as ...","date":"1 day ago","content":"1 day ago — One-Year Inflation Expectations Top 90%, Hit Record High as Middle East Risks.
